engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru XV Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru XV in the UK - including known failure causes, production years across variants, typical replacement costs, and key technical specs. All prices reflect typical UK market ranges.

cards.altText: Subaru XV FB20 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Hybrid variants with electric motor integration (2014-2018)
knownFailures: Camshaft position sensor oil contamination - affects 2013-2015 models, causes poor running.
Excessive oil consumption - reported on higher-mileage FB20 units, piston ring related.
Timing chain tensioner wear - high-mileage engines may develop rattling noise.

cards.altText: Subaru XV FB16 engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Entry-level specification across GP and GT chassis generations
knownFailures: Camshaft position sensor oil leak - same issue as FB20, affects early production units.
Oil consumption on high-mileage examples - monitor levels between services.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection variants require walnut blasting.

cards.altText: Subaru XV EE20 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Boxer diesel discontinued after 2015 due to emissions and demand
knownFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles - regeneration issues common.
Turbocharger wastegate rattle - known failure point on higher-mileage units.
EGR valve clogging - affects performance and emissions on diesel variants.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,495-£2,850 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EE20 units - actual quotes may vary.

Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise at high mileage.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru XV Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru XV's FB-series petrol engines are generally reliable but suffer from camshaft position sensor oil contamination on 2013-2015 models and oil consumption on higher-mileage units. The EE20 diesel (2012-2015) faces DPF and turbo issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. This section helps you decide between repair and replacement.

problems.group: Subaru XV Camshaft Position Sensor Oil Contamination
problems.h4: Subaru XV Camshaft Position Sensor Oil Contamination - Why FB-Series Engines Fail
problems.rootCause: Oil from the crankcase breather contaminates the camshaft position sensor electrical connector, causing intermittent signal loss, rough running, and reduced power.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Sensor Cleaning/Replacement - Early stage fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean sensor connector, replace sensor if damaged, update ECU software
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if oil contamination source not addressed; may recur

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Breather System Repair - Address root cause
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, clean breather hoses, sensor replacement, ECU update
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses contamination source; prevents recurrence if done early

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FB16/FB20 Engine - Catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded breather system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; includes preventative upgrades to prevent repeat

problems.recommendation: If caught early (rough running, check engine light), sensor cleaning and breather repair can save costs. However, if oil contamination has caused internal damage or the vehicle has high mileage, a rebuilt FB-series engine with upgraded breather components offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FB20 engine prices for your Subaru XV

problems.h4: Subaru XV Excessive Oil Consumption - FB-Series Piston Ring Issue
problems.affectedModels: Subaru XV 1.6i, 2.0i variants (2012-2020, FB16 & FB20 high-mileage)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring design allows oil to pass into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, increased oil top-ups, and potential catalytic converter damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Monitoring & Top-ups - Temporary management
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil level checks, use recommended oil grade, monitor consumption
repairOptions.longevity: Does not fix root cause; ongoing cost and risk of converter damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring Replacement - Engine-out repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble with updated components
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; success depends on cylinder condition

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FB16/FB20 with Updated Rings - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated piston ring design, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective permanent solution; includes all preventative upgrades

problems.recommendation: For XV owners experiencing oil consumption above 1 litre per 1,000 miles, replacement with a rebuilt FB-series engine featuring updated piston rings is typically more economical than an in-situ ring replacement, especially considering the labour involved and risk of incomplete repair.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned Subaru XV FB20 engine with updated piston rings

problems.group: Subaru XV 2.0D DPF and Turbocharger Failure
problems.h4: Subaru XV 2.0D DPF and Turbocharger Failure - EE20 Diesel Issues
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration, causing soot accumulation; turbo wastegate actuators wear, causing boost loss and excessive smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced regeneration, DPF chemical clean, EGR valve service
repairOptions.longevity: Works if DPF not physically damaged; requires changed driving patterns

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo or DPF Replacement - Component repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger or DPF unit, update ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses single failure point; other EE20 issues may still occur

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EE20 Diesel Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EE20 with updated turbo, DPF-ready components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution for diesel XV; includes all preventative upgrades

problems.recommendation: Given the EE20's multiple known issues (DPF, turbo, EGR) and discontinuation after 2015, owners of high-mileage diesel XVs often find better value in a rebuilt EE20 engine with all components renewed rather than repeated component repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EE20 diesel engine prices for your Subaru XV

problems.group: Subaru XV CVT Transmission Compatibility Concerns
problems.h4: Subaru XV CVT Transmission Compatibility Concerns - When Engine Replacement Intersects
problems.affectedModels: Subaru XV 2.0i CVT variants (2012-2018, FB20 with Lineartronic)
problems.rootCause: CVT fluid degradation and valve body wear can cause judder; engine replacement requires CVT compatibility verification to avoid drivetrain mismatch.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - CVT Fluid Service - Preventative maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain and fill with genuine Subaru CVT fluid, filter replacement, adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Extends CVT life if done regularly; does not fix worn components

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - CVT Valve Body Repair - Transmission rebuild
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission, replace valve body, update software, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses specific CVT fault; engine must be compatible with rebuilt unit

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Engine + CVT Compatibility Package - Integrated solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB20 engine verified for Lineartronic compatibility, CVT health check, 12-month warranty on both
repairOptions.longevity: Ensures seamless integration; ideal when both engine and transmission show wear

problems.recommendation: If your XV has both engine concerns (oil consumption, cam sensor) and CVT judder, addressing both with a compatible rebuilt engine and CVT health verification offers the most reliable path forward, avoiding costly mismatches.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Subaru XV FB20 engine with CVT compatibility verification

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru XV
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing cam sensor issues on an FB20, oil consumption on a high-mileage XV, or DPF problems on a diesel variant, EngineMarket connects you with UK Subaru XV specialists who understand boxer engine complexity. Compare prices for used, reconditioned, and rebuilt engines with warranty coverage, and choose supply-only or full supply & fit service. Get back on the road with confidence, without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru XV Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru XV and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru XV Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru XV in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Subaru XV 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre FB16 is the entry-level petrol engine for the Subaru XV, offering economical performance for urban and mixed driving. Fitted across 1.6i and 1.6i SE trims from launch (2012) through current production, it shares the FB-series architecture with the larger 2.0L but with reduced displacement for lower tax bands.
commonFailurePoints: carbon buildup on direct injection variants
items.cta: Compare Subaru XV 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru XV 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre FB20 is the most common Subaru XV engine in the UK, balancing performance and efficiency across the majority of petrol variants. Introduced at launch (2012) and continuing through current production, it powers 2.0i, SE, XE and XC trims with both manual and CVT transmissions.
commonFailurePoints: excessive oil consumption on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Subaru XV 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru XV 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre EE20 Boxer diesel was offered in the Subaru XV from 2012-2015 before discontinuation due to emissions regulations and market demand. Unique to Subaru's horizontally-opposed layout, it provided strong torque for towing and rural use but faces known DPF and turbo challenges on higher-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging affecting emissions compliance
items.cta: Get quotes for Subaru XV 2.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Subaru XV Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Subaru XV 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The hybrid variant pairs the FB20 petrol engine with an electric motor for improved efficiency in select markets. Offered 2014-2018, it maintains the FB20's mechanical architecture while adding hybrid system complexity requiring specialist diagnostic capability for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: same FB20 mechanical failures plus electrical system complexity
items.cta: Enquire about Subaru XV 2.0L hybrid engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Subaru XV needs? All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Subaru XV specialists.

Battery management system monitors state of charge and health for optimal efficiency
Replacement requires verification of both mechanical engine and hybrid electrical system compatibility
foundIn: 2.0i Hybrid, 2.0i Hybrid SE, 2.0i Hybrid XC (GT chassis 2014-2018)
importantNotes: Hybrid battery health significantly impacts overall system performance and efficiency
Specialist diagnostic equipment required for full hybrid system validation post-replacement
Availability of hybrid-specific replacement engines more limited than standard petrol variants
items.cta: Enquire about Subaru XV Hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Subaru XV Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru XV's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Subaru XV launched in the UK on the GP chassis with FB-series petrol engines and the unique EE20 Boxer diesel, establishing the compact crossover segment presence.
keyChanges: UK launch November 2012 with 1.6L FB16 and 2.0L FB20 petrol engines
EE20 Boxer diesel introduced for towing-capable variants
Symmetrical AWD standard across all variants with manual or CVT options
knownFor: First-generation XV with proven FB-series architecture replacing older EJ engines
Camshaft position sensor oil contamination issues emerge on 2013-2015 production
EE20 diesel faces early DPF regeneration challenges on short-journey usage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2017 Subaru XV replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru XV FB20 engine replacement • Subaru XV cam sensor oil leak • Subaru XV 2.0D DPF problems
enquiries: Subaru XV cam sensor oil leak

years.description: The GT chassis facelift brought updated styling, improved interior quality and refined FB-series engines with direct injection options on late-model petrol variants.
keyChanges: GT chassis launch 2017 with updated suspension, interior and safety systems
FB20D direct injection introduced on select late-model petrol variants for improved efficiency
Hybrid variants offered 2014-2018 pairing FB20 with electric motor assistance
mainEngines: FB20 - 2.0L petrol, 148-150hp (mainstream with minor efficiency updates)
FB20D - 2.0L direct injection petrol, 148hp (late-model efficiency focus)
knownFor: Improved build quality and interior refinement over GP chassis
Direct injection variants require carbon cleaning awareness on intake valves
Cam sensor issues largely resolved on post-2016 production with updated breather design
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2024 Subaru XV replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru XV FB20D direct injection • Subaru XV GT chassis engine • Subaru XV hybrid battery compatibility

years.preview: Critical Production Window for FB-Series Issues [
years.description: This narrow production window saw the emergence of camshaft position sensor oil contamination on FB16 and FB20 engines, driving significant replacement enquiries for affected XV owners.
keyChanges: Peak production years for GP chassis XV with highest UK registration volumes
Camshaft position sensor oil contamination issue identified and addressed via service bulletins
EE20 diesel faces increasing DPF and turbo-related replacement enquiries
mainEngines: FB16 - 1.6L petrol, 114hp (affected by cam sensor issue)
FB20 - 2.0L petrol, 148hp (primary engine with known oil contamination)
knownFor: Highest concentration of camshaft position sensor oil contamination cases
Oil consumption reports begin emerging on higher-mileage FB-series units
Critical period for XV owners to verify engine code and production date before ordering replacement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Subaru XV replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru XV FB20 cam sensor fix • Subaru XV oil consumption repair • Subaru XV 2014 engine replacement
enquiries: Subaru XV FB20 cam sensor fix

faq.h2: Subaru XV Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru XV engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru XV engine replacement costs typically range from £1,195-£2,850 for rebuilt units depending on engine code (FB16, FB20, EE20), with supply & fit adding £495-£895. Used engines start around £695 but carry shorter warranties.
keyPoints: FB16 1.6L petrol rebuilt: £1,195-£2,150 supply only
FB20 2.0L petrol rebuilt: £1,295-£2,450 supply only
EE20 2.0L diesel rebuilt: £1,495-£2,850 supply only (scarcer)
Supply & fit labour adds £495-£895 depending on location
All rebuilt engines include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get instant Subaru XV engine replacement quotes tailored to your variant

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru XV?
items.answer: Most UK Subaru XV models use FB16 (1.6L petrol), FB20 (2.0L petrol) or EE20 (2.0L diesel 2012-2015). Enter your registration or VIN to instantly confirm your exact engine code and compatible replacements.
keyPoints: FB16: 1.6L petrol, 114hp, 1.6i and 1.6i SE variants
FB20: 2.0L petrol, 148hp, most common UK XV engine
EE20: 2.0L diesel, 147hp, 2012-2015 only, discontinued
Use our VIN decoder or registration lookup for instant confirmation
FB-series engines are chain-driven, eliminating timing belt replacement costs
items.cta: Verify your Subaru XV engine code instantly with our compatibility checker

items.question: Is a used or reconditioned Subaru XV engine better value?
items.answer: Used XV engines cost £695-£1,850 with 3-6 month warranties; reconditioned units cost £1,195-£2,850 with 12-month unlimited mileage warranties and upgraded components addressing known FB-series failures.
keyPoints: Used: Lower upfront cost, shorter warranty, verify compression test results
Reconditioned: Higher initial cost but includes upgraded cam sensor breathers, piston rings
Reconditioned FB20 engines address oil consumption and cam sensor issues proactively
Consider total cost of ownership: reconditioned often better value long-term
All options available supply-only or with specialist fitting nationwide
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru XV engine options with warranty details

items.question: Does my Subaru XV engine replacement come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all Subaru XV engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on reconditioned/rebuilt units, with extended options available for FB20 and EE20 variants.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt engines
Extended 24-month options available for FB20 and performance variants
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage, not just supplying specialist
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month warranties; confirm before purchase
Keep service records and use recommended oil grades to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: View Subaru XV engine warranty options and upgrade to extended coverage

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru XV engine myself or do I need a specialist?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, Subaru XV boxer engines require specialist knowledge for correct mounting, CVT compatibility and ECU adaptation - we recommend using an approved Subaru specialist for supply & fit service.
keyPoints: Boxer engine layout requires specific lifting points and mounting procedures
CVT/Lineartronic compatibility verification essential to avoid drivetrain issues
ECU adaptation and immobiliser matching often required post-installation
Specialist fitting ensures warranty validity and correct post-installation validation
Supply & fit service available nationwide through approved Subaru XV specialists
items.cta: Find a Subaru XV engine fitting specialist near you with boxer expertise

items.question: Will a Subaru Impreza or Legacy engine fit my XV?
items.answer: FB-series engines (FB16, FB20) are shared across Subaru models but mounting points, ECU calibration and ancillary fittings differ - always confirm XV-specific compatibility before ordering a donor engine from another model.
keyPoints: FB16/FB20 architecture shared with Impreza, Legacy, Outback but fitment varies
Mounting points, wiring harness and ECU calibration model-specific
CVT compatibility critical - XV Lineartronic may differ from other models
Use our compatibility checker with your XV registration for precise matching
Ordering XV-specific rebuilt engines eliminates cross-model fitment risks
items.cta: Confirm Subaru XV engine compatibility before ordering from other models

items.question: How long does Subaru XV engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Most Subaru XV engines are dispatched next-day from UK stock; specialist fitting typically takes 1-2 days at approved garages, with full supply & fit turnaround usually 3-5 days from order to collection.
keyPoints: Next-day dispatch available on most FB16, FB20 and EE20 rebuilt engines
Specialist fitting takes 1-2 days depending on garage workload and complexity
CVT compatibility verification and ECU adaptation may add time
Rural locations may require additional courier time for engine delivery
Book supply & fit in advance to secure preferred fitting dates
items.cta: Check live Subaru XV engine availability and fitting timelines in your area

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Subaru XV?
items.answer: For most XV owners, engine replacement (£1,195-£2,850 rebuilt) costs significantly less than purchasing a comparable used XV (£8,000-£15,000), making replacement the economical choice for otherwise sound vehicles.
keyPoints: Rebuilt FB20 engine £1,295-£2,450 vs £8,000+ for comparable used XV
XV chassis, suspension and interior typically remain in good condition
Rebuilt engines include upgrades addressing known failure points
Warranty coverage provides peace of mind vs unknown history of used vehicle
Consider sentimental value, low mileage chassis and overall vehicle condition
items.cta: Get a personalised cost-benefit analysis for your Subaru XV engine replacement

items.question: Where is the engine code located on my Subaru XV?
items.answer: The Subaru XV engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head, visible from the front passenger side wheel arch; alternatively, check your V5C registration document or use our VIN decoder for instant identification.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block: FB16, FB20, EE20 or variant suffix
Visible from front passenger wheel arch with wheel removed for inspection
V5C registration document lists engine capacity and fuel type
VIN decoder tool cross-references chassis number to factory engine specification
When in doubt, enter your registration on our site for instant code confirmation
items.cta: Identify your Subaru XV engine code instantly with our free lookup tool

items.question: What is the most reliable engine for the Subaru XV?
items.answer: Post-2016 FB20 engines with updated breather design show improved reliability over early production; for diesel, the EE20 is robust if maintained but faces DPF challenges on short journeys - petrol FB20 remains the most recommended for UK XV owners.
keyPoints: FB20 2.0L petrol: Most common, good balance of performance and reliability
Post-2016 FB20: Updated breather design reduces cam sensor contamination risk
FB16 1.6L petrol: Economical, fewer reported issues but less power for towing
EE20 diesel: Strong torque but DPF/turbo concerns on urban usage patterns
Regular servicing with recommended oil grades critical for all FB-series longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for the most reliable Subaru XV engine options for your usage
